root_text.an7.6:1.3: Saddhādhanaṁ, sīladhanaṁ, hirīdhanaṁ, ottappadhanaṁ, sutadhanaṁ, cāgadhanaṁ, paññādhanaṁ.
root_text.an7.6:2.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ariyasāvako saddho hoti, saddahati tathāgatassa bodhiṁ:
root_text.an7.6:2.3: ‘itipi so bhagavā arahaṁ sammāsambuddho …pe… buddho bhagavā’ti.
root_text.an7.6:3.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ariyasāvako pāṇātipātā paṭivirato hoti …pe… surāmerayamajjapamādaṭṭhānā paṭivirato hoti.
root_text.an7.6:4.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ariyasāvako hirīmā hoti, hirīyati kāyaduccaritena vacīduccaritena manoduccaritena, hirīyati pāpakānaṁ akusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ samāpattiyā.
root_text.an7.6:5.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ariyasāvako ottappī hoti, ottappati kāyaduccaritena vacīduccaritena manoduccaritena, ottappati pāpakānaṁ akusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ samāpattiyā.
root_text.an7.6:6.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ariyasāvako bahussuto hoti sutadharo sutasannicayo. Ye te dhammā ādikalyāṇā majjhekalyāṇā pariyosānakalyāṇā sātthaṁ sabyañjanaṁ kevalaparipuṇṇaṁ parisuddhaṁ brahmacariyaṁ abhivadanti. Tathārūpāssa dhammā bahussutā honti dhātā vacasā paricitā manasānupekkhitā diṭṭhiyā suppaṭividdhā.
root_text.an7.6:7.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ariyasāvako vigatamalamaccherena cetasā agāraṁ ajjhāvasati muttacāgo payatapāṇi vosaggarato yācayogo dānasaṁvibhāgarato.
root_text.an7.6:8.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ariyasāvako paññavā hoti …pe… sammā dukkhakkhayagāminiyā.

translation_text.an7.6:1.1: “Mendicants, there are these seven kinds of wealth.
translation_text.an7.6:1.3: The wealth of faith, ethics, conscience, prudence, learning, generosity, and wisdom.
translation_text.an7.6:2.1: And what is the wealth of faith?
translation_text.an7.6:2.2: It’s when a noble disciple has faith in the Realized One’s awakening …
translation_text.an7.6:2.4: This is called the wealth of faith.
translation_text.an7.6:3.1: And what is the wealth of ethical conduct?
translation_text.an7.6:3.2: It’s when a noble disciple doesn’t kill living creatures, steal, commit sexual misconduct, use speech that’s false, divisive, harsh, or nonsensical, or consume beer, wine, and liquor intoxicants.
translation_text.an7.6:3.3: This is called the wealth of ethical conduct.
translation_text.an7.6:4.1: And what is the wealth of conscience?
translation_text.an7.6:4.2: It’s when a noble disciple has a conscience. They’re conscientious about bad conduct by way of body, speech, and mind, and conscientious about having any bad, unskillful qualities.
translation_text.an7.6:4.3: This is called the wealth of conscience.
translation_text.an7.6:5.1: And what is the wealth of prudence?
translation_text.an7.6:5.2: It’s when a noble disciple is prudent. They’re prudent when it comes to bad conduct by way of body, speech, and mind, and prudent when it comes to the acquiring of any bad, unskillful qualities.
translation_text.an7.6:5.3: This is called the wealth of prudence.
translation_text.an7.6:6.1: And what is the wealth of learning?
translation_text.an7.6:6.2: It’s when a noble disciple is very learned, remembering and keeping what they’ve learned. These teachings are good in the beginning, good in the middle, and good in the end, meaningful and well-phrased, describing a spiritual practice that’s entirely full and pure. They are very learned in such teachings, remembering them, rehearsing them, mentally scrutinizing them, and penetrating them theoretically.
translation_text.an7.6:6.3: This is called the wealth of learning.
translation_text.an7.6:7.1: And what is the wealth of generosity?
translation_text.an7.6:7.2: It’s when a noble disciple lives at home rid of the stain of stinginess, freely generous, open-handed, loving to let go, committed to charity, loving to give and to share.
translation_text.an7.6:7.3: This is called the wealth of generosity.
translation_text.an7.6:8.1: And what is the wealth of wisdom?
translation_text.an7.6:8.2: It’s when a noble disciple is wise. They have the wisdom of arising and passing away which is noble, penetrative, and leads to the complete ending of suffering.
translation_text.an7.6:8.3: This is called the wealth of wisdom.
translation_text.an7.6:9.1: These are the seven kinds of wealth.
translation_text.an7.6:10.1: Faith and ethical conduct are kinds of wealth,
translation_text.an7.6:10.4: and wisdom is the seventh kind of wealth.
translation_text.an7.6:11.4: their life is not in vain.
translation_text.an7.6:12.1: So let the wise devote themselves
translation_text.an7.6:12.3: confidence, and insight into the teaching,
translation_text.an7.6:12.4: remembering the instructions of the Buddhas.”
